user_query:
I need a small, verifiable script to turn our local policy content into an employee-facing FAQ and to update a draft communication. Please implement a script that runs locally (you can choose the language) to produce both of the following artifacts using only the files under input/: 1) output/faq.json and 2) output/Comms_Updated.md.

Requirements and constraints:
- Inputs:
  - Policy files: scan all Markdown files under input/policies/ (do not hardcode specific filenames). In the provided workspace these include input/policies/policy_layoffs.md and input/policies/benefits.md.
  - Questions: input/feedback/questions.jsonl (JSONL with fields id and text).
  - Contacts: input/resources/contacts.csv (columns: department,name,email).
  - Draft to rewrite: input/templates/Comms_Draft.md.
- Inspect the input/policies directory for all .md files and parse section headings defined as lines that start with "## ". For each such section, capture its content as the consecutive paragraph text until the next "## " or end of file.
- Normalize a category_key from each section heading: lowercase the heading, replace any non-alphanumeric characters with single underscores, collapse repeated underscores, and trim leading/trailing underscores (e.g., "Employee Assistance Program (EAP)" -> "employee_assistance_program_eap").
- Match questions to sections using only case-insensitive keyword presence based on the section heading text, with these explicit rules to keep it deterministic:
  - A question text matches section "Severance" if it contains the word "severance".
  - "Notice Period" matches if question contains "notice".
  - "Eligibility" matches if question contains "eligible" or "eligibility".
  - "Mental Health Support" matches if question contains the phrase "mental health".
  - "COBRA Coverage" matches if question contains "cobra".
  - "Employee Assistance Program (EAP)" matches if question contains either "employee assistance program" or "eap".
  - Assign each question to exactly one section: if multiple rules could apply, choose the section whose heading text appears earliest in the question text; if none apply, leave the question unmatched and exclude it from outputs (but this dataset should fully match).
- Generate output/faq.json with the following exact structure:
  {
    "categories": [
      {
        "category_key": string,
        "source_file": string (relative path to the .md file where the section was found),
        "answer": string (the captured paragraph text for the section),
        "questions": [string, ...] (the original question texts from input/feedback/questions.jsonl that matched this section, in file order),
        "contact_email": string
      }, ...
    ]
  }
  Rules for contact_email: derive it by reading input/resources/contacts.csv — if the source_file basename contains "layoffs", use the email for department "Employee Relations"; if it contains "benefits", use the email for department "Benefits". Do not hardcode emails; read them from contacts.csv.
  Only include categories that have at least one matched question.
- Rewrite the draft at input/templates/Comms_Draft.md by replacing the placeholder line "<!-- FAQ_SUMMARY -->" with a bullet list summary derived from the generated faq.json. For each category in faq.json, insert one line with: "- {category_key}: {count_of_questions} questions; source: {basename(source_file)}; contact: {contact_email}". Preserve all other draft content verbatim. Save the result to output/Comms_Updated.md.
- Do not modify any files under input/. Place all generated files under an output/ directory (create it if needed).

Deliverables:
- output/faq.json as specified.
- output/Comms_Updated.md with the placeholder replaced by the generated summary.

Note: This is for an employee relations update during a difficult period. Please keep the processing deterministic by following the matching rules above. The outputs should be derivable strictly from the provided inputs without external data. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
